title: Brazil’s First Ethanol-Powered Ship Sails in Win for Biofuels
summary: The first container ship to run on Brazilian-made ethanol fuel is scheduled to sail early Tuesday from the port of Santos, an unprecedented test for the nation’s biofuel industry that marks a key step toward cutting emissions in maritime transportation.

summary: Genesis Minerals Ltd. and Vault Minerals Ltd. will merge to create Australia’s third-largest gold producer, a day after rival bidder Regis Resources Ltd. said it would not counter Genesis’s bid and pulled out of the running.

title: Billionaire Birla Seals $1.8 Billion Deal for Shell's Indian Renewables Unit
summary: A unit of billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla’s conglomerate agreed to buy Shell Plc’s renewable energy assets in India for an enterprise value of 172 billion rupees ($1.8 billion) in one of the largest clean energy deals in the country.

title: Gold Miners Cadillac, Amapa Minerals Add to Canada IPO Rebound
summary: Cadillac Mines Corp. and Amapa Minerals Holdings Inc. filed for initial public offerings, in the latest sign that the pace of Canadian listings is starting to pick up.

title: Polymarket Partners With Crypto Firm During World Cup Semifinals
summary: Crypto brokerage Blockchain.com Group Holdings Inc. has partnered with Polymarket to offer prediction markets to its users in time for the World Cup semifinals.

title: Ethiopian Air Studies Large Order That Puts Jet Rivals in Play
summary: Ethiopian Airlines Group is in the final stages of deciding on an aircraft order for as many as 50 jets, as Africa’s largest carrier looks to renew its fleet and expand its foothold on the continent.

title: PBOC Boosts Liquidity to Smooth Tax Payments, Debt Issuance
summary: China’s central bank said it’s boosting longer-term liquidity injections through outright reverse repurchase agreements, a move expected to help companies and individuals handle upcoming tax payments and to sustain demand for debt issuance.

title: India Refiners Reap Fuel Export Windfall as War Drives Shortages
summary: Oil-product shipments from India are on track to climb to their highest level since September as refiners cash in on robust profit margins after a Russian export ban and hostilities in the Middle East tightened supplies.

title: Indian Activist’s Health Worsens in Hunger Strike for Students
summary: The health of a well-known Indian activist is deteriorating more than two weeks into his hunger strike over exam paper leaks, putting pressure on the government to respond to one of the country’s most prominent youth-driven protests in years.

title: Fed Hike Bets Mount Ahead of CPI Data, Warsh Hearing
summary: Bond traders ramped up bets for a July interest-rate hike ahead of US inflation data and an appearance by the head of the Federal Reserve that stand to reinforce the need for action. Bloomberg's Ven Ram reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Deutsche Bank May Win Russia-Sanctions Lawsuit Against Linde
summary: Deutsche Bank AG may win a legal battle in a dispute with a corporate client over who has to pick up the tap for losses linked to sanctions on Russia, according to a German court.

title: India’s Birla Taps MUFG for $1.5 Billion to Fund Shell Asset Buy
summary: A unit of billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla’s conglomerate has lined up around $1.5 billion of acquisition financing from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. to fund its purchase of Shell Plc’s renewable energy assets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Ed Miliband Seen as Least Appealing UK Chancellor: Markets Pulse
summary: Andy Burnham is viewed as bearish for UK assets, with former Labour Party leader Ed Miliband seen as the least market-friendly pick for Chancellor, according to Bloomberg's latest Markets Pulse Survey. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks, Treasuries Steady Before Warsh and CPI: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks steadied as traders refrained from making big bets ahead of a jam-packed day that will see the unofficial start of the earnings season, along with testimony from Fed Chair Kevin Warsh and US inflation data.

title: Pricey Stocks Charge Into CPI, Earnings and Fed, All in One Day
summary: Equities traders seem unperturbed by the dizzying array of key events on the calendar Tuesday, creating a precarious setup with the stock market hovering near a record high.

title: BofA Warns Stock Bulls to Consider Curbing Aggressive Buying
summary: Global investors buying stocks aggressively should consider reducing exposure, according to Bank of America Corp.’s fund manager survey.

title: BP Flags $1 Billion Writedown From Low Carbon Business
summary: BP Plc said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter, as the British major continues the painstaking work of re-orientating itself toward its core oil and gas business.

title: Traders Boost BOE and ECB Rate-Hike Bets After Oil Price Surge
summary: Traders boosted wagers on faster Bank of England and European Central Bank interest-rate hikes after surging oil prices reignited inflation fears.

title: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude as Iran War Intensifies
summary: The escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian refiners to look at buying more US oil.

title: Prime Data Centers Delays Sale of $500 Million Nordic Bond
summary: Data-center operator Prime Data Centers LLC has postponed a planned bond sale, in a sign of the limits of investor demand for AI exposure.

title: China Widens Australian Canola Trade to Include Private Crushers
summary: China will allow its private oilseed crushers to buy Australian canola, as the two countries move to normalize a trade that’s largely been halted since the start of the decade.

title: Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
summary: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May, supported by dollar inflows tied to SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Iran Sneaks Out Tankers Via Hormuz as Trump Amps Up Threats
summary: Iran has reverted to its well-used tactic of going dark to move oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, even before the US said it would reimpose a blockade of the country’s ports.

title: Rathbones Cuts Gilt Exposure in Case Burnham ‘Does a Truss’
summary: Rathbones Asset Management has reduced its holdings of UK government bonds to avoid a potential selloff if Andy Burnham boosts spending and raises borrowing.

title: Africa’s $219 Billion Fund Suspends CEO as Internal Rift Deepens
summary: The board of Africa’s largest state-run fund suspended Chief Executive Officer Patrick Dlamini in a late-night move that highlighted the deepening feud at the $219 billion asset manager.

title: Oil Tops $86 a Barrel as Trump Reinstates Naval Blockade on Iran
summary: Brent oil rose above $86 a barrel for the first time in a month, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and tensions rose after attacks on two tankers.

title: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
summary: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

title: SK Hynix Shares Rebound After Early Rout as AI Jitters Persist
summary: SK Hynix Inc. erased an early plunge to climb more than 3% in Seoul on Tuesday, underscoring the extreme volatility gripping one of the world’s biggest beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence boom.

title: India Price Risks Mount on Renewed US-Iran Tensions, El Nino
summary: India’s stronger-than-expected inflation reading for June has left policymakers facing a more uncertain outlook, with renewed tensions around the Strait of Hormuz threatening to push up oil prices and a developing El Niño raising the risk of higher food costs.

title: Senegal Bondholders Start Grouping as Nation Seeks Adviser
summary: Some creditors holding Senegal’s sovereign bonds have begun informal talks to potentially form a group if the government moves forward with a debt rework, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Trillion Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results
summary: At a time of acute investor unease over a selloff in AI-inflated tech stocks, this week’s results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and ASML Holding NV carry even greater significance than usual.

title: Europe Is Set for the Strongest Earnings Growth in Three Years
summary: European companies are set to post their strongest profit growth in three years in the second-quarter reporting season, buoyed by expected blowout results from oil majors, banks and artificial intelligence winners.
